title: Delicate Flaky American Bison Sliders
description: A romantic American dish featuring tender bison, flaky pastry, and a raclette grill, this recipe is paleo-friendly and perfect for a night in with loved ones.
instructions: Preheat the raclette grill to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground bison meat, almond flour, coconut oil, egg, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands until just combined.
Divide the bison mixture into 8 equal parts and shape each part into a small ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin patty using your hands or a spatula.
Roll out the flaky pastry dough to a large sheet, about 1/4 inch thick.
Use a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out small circles of pastry dough.
Place a bison patty in the center of each pastry circle, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the patty.
Top each patty with a spoonful of caramelized onions and a sprinkle of chopped thyme.
Fold the pastry dough over the filling, pressing the edges to seal, and use your hands to shape into a nigiri-like shape.
Place the sliders on the preheated raclette grill and cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the bison is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
